pairs.en: Jacob Martin Rode:Thank you, Ludovic. And now let's turn to the last two questions. For the second last question. Please go ahead, operator.
pairs.zh: 雅各布·马丁·罗德：谢谢你，卢多维克。现在让我们转向最后两个问题。对于倒数第二个问题。请继续，接线员。

pairs.en: Operator:The question comes from the line of Richard Vosser from JPMorgan.
pairs.zh: 接线员：这个问题来自摩根大通的Richard Vosser。

pairs.en: Richard Vosser:First question, Mike, you alluded to more telehealth involvement, particularly in the U.S. I think the prior arrangements, particularly with Hims, have faced challenges given they continue to bulk compound. So there's been some discussion around continuing or new agreements with Hims, but also the wider involvement of the telehealth. So I wondered what was different this time and whether there's any evidence of any increased pressure around from the FDA or legal pressure to remove the compounders and what could change on that front. And then the second question, just you mentioned a couple of times about coverage, maybe even getting a little bit worse in Medicaid and maybe even the commercial payers, the barriers staying high.
pairs.zh: 理查德·沃瑟：第一个问题，迈克，你提到了更多的远程医疗参与，特别是在美国。我认为之前的安排，特别是与希姆斯的安排，由于它们继续大量复合而面临着挑战。因此，围绕与 Hims 的持续协议或新协议进行了一些讨论，而且还讨论了远程医疗的更广泛参与。所以我想知道这次有什么不同，是否有任何证据表明 FDA 或法律压力增加了移除复合剂的压力，以及这方面可能会发生什么变化。然后是第二个问题，刚才你提到了几次关于保险范围的问题，医疗补助甚至商业付款人的情况可能会变得更糟，障碍仍然很高。

pairs.en: Well, in that case, how should we think about the pricing? Normally, we think about increased rebate levels and increased pricing should remove barriers to get more reimbursement. So how should we think about that going into '26?
pairs.zh: 那么，既然如此，我们应该如何考虑定价呢？通常，我们认为提高回扣水平和提高定价应该可以消除获得更多报销的障碍。那么进入 26 年后我们应该如何看待这一点呢？

pairs.en: Jacob Martin Rode:Thanks, Richard. On the first one, I'll turn to you, Mike, on the telehealth.
pairs.zh: 雅各布·马丁·罗德：谢谢，理查德。关于第一个问题，迈克，我将向您求助有关远程医疗的问题。

pairs.en: Maziar Doustdar:Yes. A couple of comments on that, Richard. There's been no increased pressure to answer your question directly. But what we have been quite consistent with regards to the illicit API that is coming from China and being used by compounders, we find -- since these are not FDA approved, their safety is questionable. And as a pharma company, we don't basically like that. That hasn't changed at all. We also have been saying that we need to increase our access and we need to find ways to get to many more patients. The cash channel and eHealth is definitely an attractive way to go about it.
pairs.zh: 马齐亚尔·杜斯塔：是的。对此有几点评论，理查德。直接回答你的问题并没有增加压力。但我们发现，关于来自中国并被复合商使用的非法原料药的情况一直非常一致——由于这些原料药未经 FDA 批准，其安全性值得怀疑。作为一家制药公司，我们基本上不喜欢这样。这一点都没有改变。我们还一直在说，我们需要增加我们的服务范围，我们需要找到方法来接触更多的患者。现金渠道和电子医疗绝对是一种有吸引力的方式。

pairs.en: In that context, we're having dialogue and discussions with multiple players on how we could actually increase our access and then we will see with who we should go into partnership. We've made a number of those partnerships available. So we talked about Costco. We have talked about EMed. We have talked about Walmart. And ongoing dialogues with many, many more are ongoing in pursuit of our market expansion that I spoke to earlier on.
pairs.zh: 在这种情况下，我们正在与多个参与者就如何真正增加我们的访问权限进行对话和讨论，然后我们将看看我们应该与谁建立合作伙伴关系。我们已经建立了许多这样的合作伙伴关系。所以我们谈论了好市多。我们已经讨论过 EMed。我们已经讨论过沃尔玛。为了实现我之前谈到的市场扩张，我们正在与许多人进行持续的对话。

pairs.en: Jacob Martin Rode:Thank you, Mike. That's clear. And for the second one, let's move to you again, Dave, please.
pairs.zh: 雅各布·马丁·罗德：谢谢你，迈克。很清楚。对于第二个问题，请让我们再次请您发言，戴夫。

pairs.en: David Moore:Yes. Thanks very much, Richard. We continue to have dynamics that we've discussed in the past, continued pressure, both in GLP-1 as well as in obesity with respect to price as we look to unlock more volumes. But I think it's -- I do want to reiterate that the quality of access is a clear priority for us. We are working with payers to make sure that the experience is one that patients are able to receive their medicine more seamlessly. That means lower utilization management, right? That means less of a prior authorization criteria. When we say we're investing in quality of access, that's really what we mean. And that's the focused conversation that we're having with payers.
pairs.zh: 大卫·摩尔：是的。非常感谢，理查德。我们继续保持过去讨论过的动态，即 GLP-1 和肥胖方面价格方面的持续压力，因为我们希望释放更多数量。但我认为，我确实想重申，访问质量显然是我们的首要任务。我们正在与付款人合作，确保患者能够更无缝地获得药物。这意味着利用率管理较低，对吧？这意味着更少的事先授权标准。当我们说我们正在投资于访问质量时，这正是我们的意思。这就是我们与付款人进行的重点对话。

pairs.en: In 2026, we don't really expect a large difference in terms of access in the commercial channel. As we mentioned, there may be some in Medicaid. But when we look at the commercial opportunity, I think it is important to note that we haven't been making progress in terms of the access. We continue to believe that the CVS opportunity remains there, and that will -- we will see Wegovy as the exclusive brand at AOM, at CVS in 2026 as well.
pairs.zh: 到 2026 年，我们并不真正预计商业渠道的访问量会出现很大差异。正如我们提到的，医疗补助中可能有一些。但当我们审视商业机会时，我认为重要的是要注意我们在准入方面尚未取得进展。我们仍然相信 CVS 的机会仍然存在，而且我们将在 2026 年将 Wegovy 视为 AOM 和 CVS 的独家品牌。

pairs.en: Jacob Martin Rode:Thanks a lot, Dave. That's clear. And let's move to the final question before I hand over to you, Mike, for closing. So final question, please.
pairs.zh: 雅各布·马丁·罗德：非常感谢，戴夫。很清楚。在我把问题交给迈克结束之前，让我们讨论最后一个问题。最后一个问题，请。

pairs.en: Operator:Your final question today comes from the line of James Quigley from Goldman Sachs.
pairs.zh: 接线员：您今天的最后一个问题来自高盛的詹姆斯·奎格利。

pairs.en: James Quigley:I've got two left, please. So firstly, on REDEFINE 4, how important is this into demonstrating differentiation from a physician and a marketing perspective versus Zepbound? Have you thought any more about how Zepbound could behave in a flexible dosing scenario versus other trials and real-world data? And can you confirm that you still look to launch CagriSema if REDEFINE 4 shows no statistically significant difference? And second one, can you talk through the launch preparations for Wegovy in ex U.S.? I mean you've highlighted in the release and in the comments that you could look to launch in select countries versus previously when it's likely to be mainly focused in the U.S.
pairs.zh: 詹姆斯·奎格利：我还剩两个。首先，在 REDEFINE 4 上，与 Zepbound 相比，从医生和营销角度展示差异化有多重要？您是否更多地想过 Zepbound 与其他试验和真实世界数据相比在灵活剂量场景中的表现如何？如果 REDEFINE 4 没有显示出统计上的显着差异，您能否确认您仍然希望启动 CagriSema？第二个问题，您能谈谈 Wegovy 在美国以外地区的启动准备工作吗？我的意思是，您在新闻稿和评论中强调，您可能会考虑在选定的国家/地区推出，而之前可能主要集中在美国。

pairs.en: So what's happened such that you consider also launching in the ex U.S.? And how do you balance this with U.S. launch processes and pricing, et cetera, as we head into an MFN world?
pairs.zh: 那么，发生了什么让您考虑在美国以外地区推出业务？当我们进入最惠国待遇世界时，您如何平衡这一点与美国的发布流程和定价等？

pairs.en: Jacob Martin Rode:Thanks, James. That's two. One on the REDEFINE 4 and one on the orals sema in IO. But on the REDEFINE one, I'll turn it to you first, Martin.
pairs.zh: 雅各布·马丁·罗德：谢谢，詹姆斯。那是两个。一份在 REDEFINE 4 上，一份在 IO 中的口腔塞玛上。但关于 REDEFINE 的问题，我会先交给你，马丁。

pairs.en: Martin Holst Lange:Yes, REDEFINE 4. As you well know, we've taken some learnings from REDEFINE 1 more specifically that we needed to do a lumber study. So we amended the REDEFINE 4 study. That being said, our base case has always been non-inferiority with an upside of superiority. In the case of non-inferiority on weight loss, we still see a potential for upside on gastrointestinal side effects and tolerability. But also we do believe that the CV benefits that we know from semaglutide could also potentially pan out and will read out from REDEFINE 3, thus clearly differentiate CagriSema vis-a-vis potential competitors. But I do want to iterate, there is still the potential for superiority upside.
pairs.zh: Martin Holst Lange：是​​的，REDEFINE 4。正如您所知，我们从 REDEFINE 1 中学到了一些更具体的知识，我们需要进行木材研究。因此我们修改了 REDEFINE 4 研究。话虽这么说，我们的基本情况始终是非劣势且具有优势。在减肥的非劣效性的情况下，我们仍然看到胃肠道副作用和耐受性有上升的潜力。但我们也确实相信，我们从 semaglutide 中了解到的 CV 优势也可能会发挥作用，并将从 REDEFINE 3 中读出，从而使 CagriSema 与潜在竞争对手明显区分开来。但我确实想重申，仍然存在优势上升的潜力。

pairs.en: But obviously in an amended study, that should be taken with a little bit of a risk.
pairs.zh: 但显然，在一项修改后的研究中，这应该冒一点风险。

pairs.en: Jacob Martin Rode:And let's turn to you, Ludovic, for the second one, please.
pairs.zh: 雅各布·马丁·罗德（Jacob Martin Rode）：卢多维奇，请让我们请您讲第二个问题。

pairs.en: Ludovic Helfgott:Absolutely. So let's take a step back. The real focus and the priority of our Wegovy pill launch is the U.S. and will remain the U.S. in 2026. As you rightly read, we are indeed opening -- we've filed, and we are opening the option to launch in selected markets in the course of 2026, depending, of course, on how things are ramping up, and we are definitely ready to -- in this market to be able to make the -- the best and the most of the Wegovy pill. By the way, we're also very quickly transferring some knowledge from the telehealth channels, for instance, in the U.S. into some higher markets.
pairs.zh: 卢多维克·赫尔夫戈特：当然。那么让我们退后一步。我们 Wegovy 药丸上市的真正焦点和优先事项是美国，并且到 2026 年仍将在美国。正如您所读的那样，我们确实正在开放——我们已经提交了申请，并且我们正在开放在 2026 年期间在选定市场上市的选择，当然，这取决于事情的进展情况，我们绝对准备好——在这个市场上能够制造出——最好和最多的 Wegovy 药丸。顺便说一句，我们也很快将一些知识从美国的远程医疗渠道转移到一些更高的市场。

pairs.en: We are very active right now as we speak in many markets across the world, not just in Europe, but also in Australia, for instance, or in other part of the world where we believe that we really are gaining progress on the telehealth side. So again, we're accelerating our overall experience curve on the telehealth and that will be -- we believe will be proven very helpful at the time we launch the Wegovy pill.
pairs.zh: 我们现在非常活跃，我们在世界各地的许多市场上发言，不仅在欧洲，而且在澳大利亚，或者在世界其他地区，我们相信我们在远程医疗方面确实取得了进展。因此，我们再次加快了远程医疗的整体经验曲线，我们相信，在我们推出 Wegovy 药丸时，这将被证明非常有帮助。

pairs.en: It's also not worthy to say that the IO market are also markets where we have some of the nicest experience in Rybelsus in Europe and elsewhere, which means that we can also leverage our experience in the oral markets that have been successful for Rybelsus to build even quicker position with the Wegovy pill. So priority to the U.S., but fully ready to take on some selected markets in IO when time comes.
pairs.zh: 也不值得说，IO 市场也是我们在欧洲和其他地方拥有 Rybelsus 一些最好经验的市场，这意味着我们还可以利用我们在 Rybelsus 成功的口服市场中的经验，通过 Wegovy 药丸更快地建立地位。因此优先考虑美国，但时机成熟时，已做好充分准备进入 IO 的某些选定市场。

pairs.en: Jacob Martin Rode:Thanks, Ludovic. And with that, let's conclude the Q&A session. Thank you for participating, and please feel free to ask in the Investor Relations for any follow-up questions if that's case. Before we finally round off, I'll just turn it over to you, Mike, for closing.
pairs.zh: 雅各布·马丁·罗德：谢谢，卢多维克。至此，我们就结束问答环节了。感谢您的参与，如有任何后续问题，请随时在投资者关系中询问。在我们最终结束之前，迈克，我将把它交给你来结束。

pairs.en: Maziar Doustdar:Thank you, Jacob. Thank you to everyone for calling in. Before closing, I did want to make a few remarks. Although we have narrowed the full year outlook for this year and see currently competitive headwind, the unmet need is huge. The volume opportunity longer term in our core area is enormous. We are sharpening our focus on diabetes, obesity and associated comorbidities in order to address this unmet need. This is our home turf, and this is for us to drive the commercial execution and raise the innovation bar to treat many more patients and treat them better than ever before, and we will not stop until we do that. Thank you.
pairs.zh: Maziar Doustdar：谢谢你，雅各布。感谢大家的来电。在结束之前，我确实想说几句话。尽管我们缩小了今年的全年展望，并看到了当前的竞争逆风，但未满足的需求是巨大的。从长远来看，我们核心领域的销量机会是巨大的。我们正在加强对糖尿病、肥胖症和相关合并症的关注，以解决这一未满足的需求。这是我们的主场，我们要推动商业执行并提高创新标准，以治疗更多患者并比以往更好地治疗他们，除非我们做到这一点，否则我们不会停止。谢谢。

pairs.en: Operator:Thank you. This concludes today's conference call. Thank you for participating. You may now disconnect.
pairs.zh: 接线员：谢谢。今天的电话会议到此结束。感谢您的参与。您现在可以断开连接。
